issue_title: Yolk crashes when PyPi package descriptions contain certain characters
issue_body:
Yolk (0.8.7) [reports and encoding error](http://stackoverflow.com/q/33860028/656912) when printing information about packages. 

For example, when I

    yolk -M crypto-enigma

I get

> UnicodeEncodeError: 'ascii' codec can't encode character u'\u03b2' in
> position 2322: ordinal not in range(128)

because the [PyPi page for the package](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/crypto-enigma) has a `β` in it. Or if I try

    yolk -M Metafone

I get

> UnicodeEncodeError: 'ascii' codec can't encode characters in position 3291-3292: ordinal not in range(128)

Is there a way to avoid this error? It seems like a bug that Yolk would crash just because there's a Greek character or mathematical symbol in the PyPi page for a package.

issue_title: Transaction details parsing error if content is unfortunately wrapped
issue_body:
Hi guys,

I discovered a problem with the parsing method of the transaction model.
Actually this error prevents me fetching transactions with fints.
In the example below (obfuscated) the transaction details ":86:" contain an  ISO timetamp which is
unfortunately wrapped to the next line. The ":26:" is no tag, but the minutes of the ISO timestamp.

>:61:000000D8,47NMSCNONREF
:86:106?000000/661?20EREF+VZ0000000000000000?21MREF+000000?22CRED+XX0
000000000000000?23ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVW?24/PL 12-09-2014T16
:26:37  Fo?25lgenr. 007

The parser raises an exception since it doesn't know the tag ":26:" (it doesn't exist either). 
The root cause (I guess) is the method used to extract the tags (models.py:333). 
Basically it searches for lines starting with ':XX:' which includes also not existing 'tags'.

It better way may be to search for known tags by listing them in the regex like:
`r'^:(?P<full_tag>61|86|...|...):'`

This still would lead to possible parsing errors for tags below 60, so maybe another parsing strategy is more appropriate.

issue_title: possible issue: gdbgui may freeze with very large files
issue_body: gdbgui may slow and exit if the binary file has a large number of source files. Output from the command `-file-list-exec-source-files` is very verbose, and parser-intensive. It might be a good idea to not run that command if the binary to debug is very large, or just just add a button that refreshes the source file list. The line in question is: https://github.com/cs01/gdbgui/blob/master/gdbgui/static/js/gdbgui.js#L1638

issue_title: Non utf-8 results exception
issue_body:
If in results object we have some non utf-8 characters we get this exception:
```
[E 150728 15:51:29 web:1421] Uncaught exception GET /api/task/result/f0209a45-1b09-42b7-a284-5d4d5082b10a (127.0.0.1)
    HTTPServerRequest(protocol='http', host='127.0.0.1:5555', method='GET', uri='/api/task/result/f0209a45-1b09-42b7-a284-5d4d5082b10a', version='HTTP/1.1', remote_ip='127.0.0.1', headers={'Host': '127.0.0.1:5555', 'Accept': '*/*', 'User-Agent': 'curl/7.38.0'})
    Traceback (most recent call last):
      File "<env>\lib\site-packages\tornado\web.py", line 1346, in _execute
        result = method(*self.path_args, **self.path_kwargs)
      File "<env>\lib\site-packages\tornado\web.py", line 2626, in wrapper
        return method(self, *args, **kwargs)
      File "<src>\flower\flower\api\tasks.py", line 314, in get
        self.update_response_result(response, result)
      File "<src>\flower\flower\api\tasks.py", line 51, in update_response_result
        response.update({'result': self.safe_result(result.result)})
      File "<src>\flower\flower\api\tasks.py", line 72, in safe_result
        json.dumps(result)
      File "C:\Python27\Lib\json\__init__.py", line 243, in dumps
        return _default_encoder.encode(obj)
      File "C:\Python27\Lib\json\encoder.py", line 207, in encode
        chunks = self.iterencode(o, _one_shot=True)
      File "C:\Python27\Lib\json\encoder.py", line 270, in iterencode
        return _iterencode(o, 0)
    UnicodeDecodeError: 'utf8' codec can't decode byte 0xfc in position 52: invalid start byte
```

My fix would be:
```python
def safe_result(self, result):
        "returns json encodable result"
        try:
            json.dumps(result)
        except TypeError:
            return repr(result)
        except UnicodeDecodeError:
            result_reencoded = unicode(json.dumps(result, ensure_ascii=False), errors='replace').encode("ascii", "replace")
            return json.loads(result_reencoded)
        else:
            return result
```

because tornado doesn't support non utf-8 encoding too...

issue_title: generated documentation and link to online generator
issue_body:
When needing a to add a new extension to glad, it is necessary to return to the generator. Unfortunately, this then requires reselecting all of the prior options manually, and (if you aren't keeping good documentation) trial-and-error to make sure you got all of the ones you need.

It would be extremely convenient if the generated source/header contained a comment that both (a) detailed clearly the specific options used in generation, (b) contained a command line that could be used to regenerate that exact source, and (c) contained a link to the online generator that would prefill all the corresponding options.

Of course, the online generator would need to support a way to set selected items via the URL itself for that last bit to actually be useful.

Something like this in the beginning of the generated `glad.h`:

    /* Generated OpenGL Loader
       by glad 0.1.8a on the 10th of Oct, 2015
         https://github.com/Dav1dde/glad
       License: ... what is the license of the generated output, anyway?
       Options:
         OpenGL 3.3 (Core)
         GL_ARB_sync
         GL_ARB_texture_barrier
         ...
       Command:
         glad --profile core --api gl=3.3 --generator c --spec gl --extensions GL_ARB_sync,GL_ARB_texture_barrier
       Online:
         http://glad.dav1d.de/?profile=core&api=gl%263.3&generator=c&extensions=GL_ARB_sync,GL_ARB_texture_barrier
    */

My current workaround is to have a master registry singleton that takes a registry and return an unified registry. The master class knows the used registry and if the input registry is similar another one already in the known registry list, the registry from the database is returned. 

```
a = pint.UnitRegistry()
b = pint.UnitRegistry()
c = Registry(a)
d = Registry(b)
e = c.meters * d.meters
```

I don't like this workaround. Is there any better solution?

issue_title: SearchEndpoint cannot handle args with '+' character
issue_body:
I was testing the Zenpy client today with a gmail address that has a '+' character in it, and I couldn't figure out why it was returning no results. After some digging, I found this:

```python
from zenpy.lib import endpoint
e = endpoint.EndpointFactory('search')
print e('mfwmtesting+201803140@gmail.com', type='ticket')
# ==> 'search.json?query=mfwmtesting+201803140@gmail.com+type:"ticket"'
```

It seems to me that the issue is that the _args_ are not getting properly URL encoded, and the "+" in my email address is being interpreted as a space character in the query parameter. Furthermore, the [ZD API reference](https://developer.zendesk.com/rest_api/docs/core/search#url-encoding-the-search-string) states that the entire search string should be URL encoded. It appears that the code is trying to do that, however it seems to miss encoding (at least) the _args_. 

**ZenPy Version:** 1.2.6

@@	Sepal.Length	Sepal.Width	Petal.Length	Petal.Width	Species

	5.7	2.8	4.1	1.3	versicolor
->	6.3	3.3	6	2.5	virginica->XXX
+++	5.8	2.7	5.1	1.9	XXX
---	5.8	2.7	5.1	1.9	virginica
->	7.1	3	5.9	2.1	virginica->XXX
->	6.3	2.9	5.6	1.8	virginica->XXX

issue_title: Installation does not happen smoothly for Jupyter (iPython 4)
issue_body: Post 3.x iPython has been split off into a variety of pieces, handling installation here is a bit problematic. There is not a clear way to handle this with `setuptools` ATM. ( https://bitbucket.org/pypa/setuptools/issues/434/handling-either-or-requirement ) However, this problem has showed up in other projects before. Likely, we can try to import iPython and see if it is < 4.0.0. If so, we can assume we have everything. If it can't be imported or it is 4.x, we will add more install requirements to solve this problem.

issue_title: Class name detection fails for metaclass
issue_body:
green reports an error when running a test case which uses a metaclasses, and also reports the wrong name for the test class. (see `__doc__` and `green.result` appearing below)

```
$ green -vvv tests.tools_tests
tests: max_retries reduced from 25 to 1
Green 2.0.7, Coverage 3.7, Python 2.7.5

tests.tools_tests
  ContextManagerWrapperTestCase
.   Check that the wrapper permits exceptions.
.   Create a test instance and verify the wrapper redirects.
.....
  __doc__
.   Test getargspec on args.
.   Test getargspec on kwargs.
.   Test getargspec on varargs.
.   Test getargspec on varkwargs.
.   Test getargspec on vars.
.....
green.result
  ProtoTestResult
.   Test getargspec on kwargs.
.   Test getargspec on varkwargs.
    errors[],Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/bin/green", line 9, in <module>
    load_entry_point('green==2.0.7', 'console_scripts', 'green')()
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/site-packages/green/cmdline.py", line 75, in main
    result = run(test_suite, stream, args, testing)
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/site-packages/green/runner.py", line 118, in run
    result.addProtoTestResult(proto_test_result)
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/site-packages/green/result.py", line 337, in addProtoTestResult
    for test, err in proto_test_result.errors:
AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'errors'
<type 'exceptions.AttributeError'>
CRITICAL: Closing network session.
```

The top level entry `  __doc__` in the above is a metaclassed test class, and it is failing to get the class name/docstring correct, but otherwise works OK:
https://github.com/wikimedia/pywikibot-core/blob/master/tests/tools_tests.py#L521

Then the failure occurs on the 'next' test class, which inherits from the metaclass

https://github.com/wikimedia/pywikibot-core/blob/master/tests/tools_tests.py#L555

Returning to the `__doc__` entry, I can fix that by sorting the names of the methods being created in `__new__`, e.g.

```diff
diff --git a/tests/tools_tests.py b/tests/tools_tests.py
index c58e33f..bdccb2f 100644
--- a/tests/tools_tests.py
+++ b/tests/tools_tests.py
@@ -506,7 +506,8 @@ class MetaTestArgSpec(MetaTestCaseClass):
                 self.assertNoDeprecation()
             return test_method
 
-        for name, tested_method in list(dct.items()):
+        for name in sorted(list(dct.keys())):
+            tested_method = dct[name]
             if name.startswith('_method_test_'):
                 suffix = name[len('_method_test_'):]
                 cls.add_method(dct, 'test_method_' + suffix,
```

causes the `green` output to be

```
...
  getargspec
.   Test getargspec on args.
.   Test getargspec on kwargs.
.   Test getargspec on varargs.
.   Test getargspec on varkwargs.
.   Test getargspec on vars.
...
```

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python

import time
from PyMata.pymata import PyMata
import os

port = PyMata(os.environ['ARDUINO_PORT'])
port.i2c_config(0, port.ANALOG, 4, 5)

try:
  while True:
    port.i2c_read(0x23, 0, 2, port.I2C_READ)
    time.sleep(3)  # wait for data to be sent

    data = port.i2c_get_read_data(0x23)
    print 'got data', data
    lux = (data[1] << 8 | data[2]) >> 4

except KeyboardInterrupt:

issue_title: The facial shape predictor fails miserably when the face is rotated a bit too much - improvement proposal
issue_body:
Hi Davis!

As you know, I have been trying to get around this issue by training a new face predictor model, and that did not work. If my understanding is correct, this is a limitation of the algorithm.

1) Anyway, I fixed this with a very small and fast convnet. If you're interested, you can read about it here http://vermeille.fr/dotclear2/index.php/post/39-Face-Recognition-at-scale-%282/N%29 . Dead simple, dead fast, and if you're interested, I can give you the weights (but they're for tf, so I'm not sure if that's of any use to you).

2) Though I'm concerned. To integrate that step in the pipeline you provide, I'm just rotating the face in its bounding box (see what I mean on the blog post). That works wonders but I stumbled upon [those lines](https://github.com/davisking/dlib/blob/0f169ed71f72faaeee9f9e44143553705b3fd4cf/dlib/image_transforms/interpolation.h#L2071) and I'm wondering if I have to actually make the area I'm rotating bigger, because it seems you are increasing the bounding box. If so, I just don't get those equations. What's the logic behind it? Reading the textual description it seems to be something like `bb_width += padding * bb_width; bb_height += bb_height * padding` but your equations seems more sophisticated.

Thanks for your quick replies and your amazing work :)

issue_title: bitrate is in bps (not kbps) for VBR mp3 files
issue_body:
Hi,

Any VBR mp3 file I point tinytag at gives me a bitrate value which is about 1000x what audio software reports. I expect that this value is bps rather than kbps.

For example, for the file at (https antisol dot org slash brass dot mp3), tinytag gives me a bitrate value of 195206.785168, whereas various audio software reports the file as being 195kbps, including:
* audacious
* qmmp
* vlc ('input bitrate' on 'statistics' screen lists ~195kbps, but codec info tab shows 128kbps)

I've seen this with all other VBR mp3 files I've tried, they all give a value ~1000x the value reported by audacious.

As a workaround I was able to do something like:
````python
if bitrate > 320: bitrate = bitrate / 1099
````
to get a pretty good bitrate value. But it would be nice to see a more accurate value come from TinyTag.

Thanks!

issue_title: Not all processors are registered
issue_body:
The registration of Processors currently depends on their instantiation. This causes some types to be registered multiple times while others are missing.

Solution: Add a configuration to MADAM which contains a list of available processors.

issue_title: namecheap authentication fails for delegated domain management
issue_body:
The following lines check to see if the domain name is returned by Namecheap's API

https://github.com/AnalogJ/lexicon/blob/master/lexicon/providers/namecheap.py#L54-L63

Namecheap's API seems to be halfassed and this won't work as expected.

please bear with me on this, it's going to be odd to explain.

The only domains that seem to appear in this list are ones which are OWNED by the authenticating account. The list does not include items that are MANAGED by the authenticating account.  Namecheap's backend provisioning systems use an ACL (Access Control List) that allows for an domain "Owner" to proxy/delegate different responsibilities for a domain to other users of their system.

In my case, "Account1" is the `owner` of all domains, while "Account2" has been delegated admin privileges for **only** the DNS settings of a subset of domains.  This was done to allow a tier of company staff to manage DNS via the control panel AND also enable API access -- but keeping the staff and API away from registration/transfer/etc operations.

Privileged domains don't seem to appear on this paginated view and will trigger lexicon exiting.

A potential check would be the `namecheap.domains.getinfo` endpoint (https://www.namecheap.com/support/api/methods/domains/get-info.aspx)

Supplying a `&DomainName=example.com` argument will result in a valid response if the user has permissions:

```<ApiResponse Status="OK" xmlns="http://api.namecheap.com/xml.response">
  <Errors />
  <Warnings />
  <RequestedCommand>namecheap.domains.getinfo</RequestedCommand>
  <CommandResponse Type="namecheap.domains.getInfo">
    <DomainGetInfoResult Status="Ok" ID="11111111111" DomainName="EXAMPLE.COM" OwnerName="USERNAME" IsOwner="false" IsPremium="false">
      <DomainDetails>
        <CreatedDate>02/15/2000</CreatedDate>
        <ExpiredDate>02/15/3000</ExpiredDate>
        <NumYears>0</NumYears>
      </DomainDetails>
      <LockDetails />
      <Whoisguard Enabled="False">
        <ID>0</ID>
      </Whoisguard>
      <PremiumDnsSubscription>
        <UseAutoRenew>false</UseAutoRenew>
        <SubscriptionId>-1</SubscriptionId>
        <CreatedDate>0001-01-01T00:00:00</CreatedDate>
        <ExpirationDate>0001-01-01T00:00:00</ExpirationDate>
        <IsActive>false</IsActive>
      </PremiumDnsSubscription>
      <DnsDetails ProviderType="FREE" IsUsingOurDNS="true" HostCount="5" EmailType="No Email Service" DynamicDNSStatus="false" IsFailover="false">
        <Nameserver>dns1.registrar-servers.com</Nameserver>
        <Nameserver>dns2.registrar-servers.com</Nameserver>
      </DnsDetails>
      <Modificationrights All="false">
        <Rights Type="hosts">OK</Rights>
      </Modificationrights>
    </DomainGetInfoResult>
  </CommandResponse>
  <Server>PHX01APIEXT03</Server>
  <GMTTimeDifference>--5:00</GMTTimeDifference>
  <ExecutionTime>0.384</ExecutionTime>
</ApiResponse>```

Note this section which correlates to the ACL for `hosts` being enabled:

```
      <Modificationrights All="false">
        <Rights Type="hosts">OK</Rights>
      </Modificationrights>
```

If a user requests this endpoint on a domain they are not permissioned for, it's an error:

```<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<ApiResponse Status="ERROR" xmlns="http://api.namecheap.com/xml.response">
  <Errors>
    <Error Number="4011103">Access Denied</Error>
  </Errors>
  <Warnings />
  <RequestedCommand>namecheap.domains.getinfo</RequestedCommand>
  <CommandResponse Type="namecheap.domains.getInfo">
    <DomainGetInfoResult ID="0" IsOwner="false" IsPremium="false">
      <DomainDetails>
        <NumYears>0</NumYears>
      </DomainDetails>
      <LockDetails />
      <Whoisguard>
        <ID>0</ID>
      </Whoisguard>
      <PremiumDnsSubscription>
        <UseAutoRenew>false</UseAutoRenew>
        <SubscriptionId>-1</SubscriptionId>
        <CreatedDate>0001-01-01T00:00:00</CreatedDate>
        <ExpirationDate>0001-01-01T00:00:00</ExpirationDate>
        <IsActive>false</IsActive>
      </PremiumDnsSubscription>
      <DnsDetails IsUsingOurDNS="false" HostCount="0" DynamicDNSStatus="false" IsFailover="false" />
      <Modificationrights />
    </DomainGetInfoResult>
  </CommandResponse>
  <Server>PHX01APIEXT02</Server>
  <GMTTimeDifference>--5:00</GMTTimeDifference>
  <ExecutionTime>0.114</ExecutionTime>
</ApiResponse>```

i don't know enough of their system to understand all the edge cases of what valid dns rights would look like in a variety of situations, but just doing a simple error check made this library usable for me.

issue_title: Winston Lutz : underestimated bb_shift_vector value
issue_body:
Hi James, 

I really appreciate the new features that you introduced since our last discussions. I am discovering and testing the new features as part of a new QA procedure that I am working on. 

The bb_shift_vector function is very usefull, but it gives slightly inaccurate results.   
```
  def bb_shift_vector(self):
        """The shift necessary to place the BB at the radiation isocenter"""
        tv = Vector()
        vs = [img.cax2bb_vector3d for img in self.images]
        for v in vs:
            tv += v
        return Vector(-tv.x / len(vs), -tv.y / len(vs), -tv.z / len(vs))

```
Now let's assume that you have a bb positioned 1mm UP from the "real isocenter" in a 4 gantry angle Winston Lutz (0, 90,180 and 270). The cax2bb_vector3d would indeed be 1mm for 2 of the 4 gantry angles, but would be 0 mm for the images taken at gantry = 0 and 180 degrees. In consequence, the  bb_shift_vector would be 0.5 mm instead of the expected 1mm. 

The problem is simply that cax2bb_vector3d returns "0" when it simply cannot evaluates the position in a specific axis. This function could output a "NAN"... or you could modulate a summation weight on "tv" based on the sine/cosine of the gantry angle. 

Do you come to the same conclusions ? What is your opinion ?

                raise SchemaError('%s(%r) should evaluate to True' % (f, data), e)
            if s == data:
                return data

    class Optional(Schema):

        """Marker for an optional part of Schema."""

And then nothing more.

issue_title: default request matching : order of parameters in URL
issue_body:
Hi

For the default URL request matcher, does the order of the query string parameters matter?

During a second run (fetching from cassette), we get a
CannotOverwriteExistingCassetteException
(match_on = ['method', 'query'])

We noticed that the order of parameters in the URI is sometimes different. 
Would vcrpy see these requests as different? 

For example:
http://some_host.com/get_some?a=1&b=2
http://some_host.com/get_some?b=2&a=1

If vcrpy treats these as different URI,do  we need to write a custom request matcher, or can we tell vcrpy to ignore order?

python 3.4.2
vcrpy 1.10.5

Thanks!

Serge Franchosi

```python
from endpoints import register

issue_title: Support for Factory Boy / DjangoModelFactory
issue_body:
It would be great if Jedi would support DjangoModelFactory (from Factory Boy,
http://factoryboy.readthedocs.org/en/latest/recipes.html):

    # models.py
    class User(models.Model):
        first_name = models.CharField()
        group = models.ForeignKey(Group)


    # factories.py
    import factory
    from . import models

    class UserFactory(factory.django.DjangoModelFactory):
        class Meta:
            model = models.User

        first_name = factory.Sequence(lambda n: "Agent %03d" % n)
        group = factory.SubFactory(GroupFactory)

The model / class being returned is named / referred to in `Meta.model`.

It's also supported to refer to the model as a string, e.g. `'models.User'`.

Currently Jedi completes the properties of the factory class, but not the
object that is being created through calling it:

    user = UserFactory()

Here is the `__call__` method: https://github.com/rbarrois/factory_boy/blob/master/factory/base.py#L72

issue_title: Add parsing of pass-by-reference parameters
issue_body:
I know this is a C++ feature, but the patch required is only 2 lines, so I thought I'd check.

I often encounter headers written for MSVC that are mostly C, with the occasional C++-ism. I usually do some preprocessing to normalize them before sending to pycparser, but this is pretty difficult to do for the pass-by-reference syntax without essentially writing a parser.

What I'd like to do is to simply interpret pass-by-reference as pass-by-pointer (e.g. parse `func(int &x);` as `func(int *x);`. This can be done by adding two lines to the `pointer` production so that it reads:

```
pointer : TIMES type_qualifier_list_opt
        | TIMES type_qualifier_list_opt pointer
        | AND type_qualifier_list_opt
        | AND type_qualifier_list_opt pointer
```

issue_title: datetime.now() and datetime.utcnow() give different results depending on where imports are
issue_body:
datetime.now() and datetime.utcnow() seem to give different results depending on where the imports are when using freezegun. I'm using freezegun 0.3.9 and python 2.7, here's a script to recreate the issue:

```
def test_freezegun():           
    
    from freezegun import freeze_time
    from datetime import datetime, timedelta
    
    print "\nTESTING IMPORTS INSIDE FUNCTION" 
    initial_datetime = datetime.now().replace(hour=9, minute=1) + timedelta(days=1)
    
    with freeze_time(initial_datetime, tz_offset=0):
        print "datime.now(): %s" % datetime.now()
        print "datetime.utcnow(): %s" % datetime.utcnow()
        print "datime.today(): %s\n" % datetime.today()
    
test_freezegun()

from freezegun import freeze_time
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

print "TESTING IMPORTS INSIDE MODULE"
initial_datetime = datetime.now().replace(hour=9, minute=1) + timedelta(days=1)

with freeze_time(initial_datetime, tz_offset=0):
    print "datime.now(): %s" % datetime.now()
    print "datetime.utcnow(): %s" % datetime.utcnow()
    print "datime.today(): %s\n" % datetime.today()
```

And here's my output:

```
TESTING IMPORTS INSIDE FUNCTION
datime.now(): 2017-11-02 13:06:50.776239
datetime.utcnow(): 2017-11-02 13:06:50.776260
datime.today(): 2017-11-03 09:01:50.773444

TESTING IMPORTS INSIDE MODULE
datime.now(): 2017-11-03 09:01:50.776375
datetime.utcnow(): 2017-11-03 09:01:50.776375
datime.today(): 2017-11-03 09:01:50.776375
```

issue_title: netlink attribute payload byteorder issue on bigendian machines
issue_body:
There seems to be some issue with nla messages on big-endian machines. When running a simple script like this (pyroute2 0.3.6):
```python
from pyroute2 import IPRSocket

ip = IPRSocket()
ip.bind()
while 1:
  print "%s" % ip.get()
ip.close()
```
I occasionally get traces like the one below:
```
WARNING:root:decoding NDA_DST
WARNING:root:Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "build/bdist.linux-ppc64/egg/pyroute2/netlink/__init__.py", line 1084, in decode_nlas
    nla.decode()
  File "build/bdist.linux-ppc64/egg/pyroute2/netlink/__init__.py", line 1215, in decode
    family = self.family_map[self.parent['family']]
KeyError: 33554432
```
This happens when fmt in pyrote2.netlink.nlmsg_base.decode() is 'i', I've also looked at raw data in that message and it is `0x02 0x00 0x00 0x00`, so a little-endian 2.
It looks like pyroute2 expects NLAs to be in host's byte-order, but at least according to [this](http://linux-development-for-fresher.blogspot.cz/2012/05/understanding-netlink-socket.html) it is possible for payload to be in network byte-order.

issue_title: IProute.route("add", "fc00::1", oif=1) goes to default IP if no prefix len
issue_body:
Hi,

I spotted the following bug with pyroute2 from master : 

```py
ipr = IPRoute()
ipr.route('add', dst="fc00::1/16", oif=1)
ipr.route('add', dst="fc00::2", oif=1)
```
will produce from `ip -6 route` : 
```
unreachable fc00::/16 dev lo proto static metric 1024 linkdown error -101 pref medium
unreachable default dev lo proto static metric 1024 linkdown error -101 pref medium
```

The first line works fine. The second bugs, as it changes the IPv6 address "fc00::2" to default. Quite an insidious bug, took me some time to track it down :)

issue_title: Taking of screenshot (gl_readpixels) degrades performance for large figures
issue_body:
```
On a full-screen figure a call to gl_readpixels takes a lot of time, even on 
modern hardwar. We even got a MemoryError on a very new PC.

Such a call is currently made in two places. 1) in the taking of a screenshot 
of the shapes of objects (so visvis knows which object is where and can handle 
events apropriately); 2) each axes makes a screenshot and
reuses that if it does not need any updating of its contents.

The latter will probably be fixed when we come around issue 41. The first might 
be more difficult to overcome, but maybe things can improved, for instance by 
reading just a single pixel when needed instead of reading the entire screen on 
every draw.

On the short term, while these issues are still there, we need a way of 
disabling taking the screenshot at the cost of user interaction being disabled 
and axes not being able to re-use their content, respectively. The second case 
can already be disabled by doing Axes.useBuffer = False.


```

Original issue reported on code.google.com by `almar.klein@gmail.com` on 15 Aug 2012 at 2:08

issue_title: Add additional values to API_VERSIONS for 0.10.x
issue_body:
Currently [`API_VERSIONS`](https://github.com/dpkp/kafka-python/blob/master/kafka/client_async.py#L78)only has one value for 0.10 brokers. 

For safety, I'm trying to pin the Kafka broker API versions in our code. 
If there's a future 0.10.x release that breaks an API, there's no way I can demarcate that our code wasn't expecting anything newer than 0.10.0 API or 0.10.1 API version... 

Should we add values for those?

Also, It seems tedious to be adding values every time a new Kafka release happens. Is there something I'm missing? Like can I specify `(0, 10, 0)` in my code? And if an api changes, kafka-python will understand that that means 0.10.0.x?

issue_title: Handle responses / resources as they come in
issue_body:
It would be nice to have an officially-supported API for incremental responses. Right now it's only possible to wait for page loads and specific selectors. I'd like to use ghost to instrument arbitrary page loads, looking at all resources loaded as they come in, for some period of time.

Right now I'm using something like:

```
def resources(session, url, **kwargs):
    kwargs['wait'] = False
    timeout = kwargs.pop('wait_timeout', session.wait_timeout)
    session.open(url, **kwargs)

    last_update = time()
    while time() - last_update < timeout:
        for resource in session._release_last_resources():
            last_update = time()
            yield resource
        session.sleep()

    if not session.loaded:
        raise TimeoutError

for resource in resources(session, url): ...
```

Which works, but uses the `_release_last_resources` private API.

issue_title: Using settrace
issue_body:
The documentation says, under "things that don't work":

> sys.settrace is the Python feature that coverage.py uses to see what’s happening in your program. If another part of your program is using sys.settrace, then it will conflict with coverage.py, and it won’t be measured properly.

And this is of course correct. That said... Is there a way to both do coverage measurement and also perform some package-specific tracing at the same time? In my case, I have a module which needs to do some tracing for its functionality, and I'd also like to have it (not to mention applications using it) be able to measure coverage.

I tried to naively do something along the following lines:

```python
prev_tracefunc = sys.settrace(my_tracefunc)
... run some code with my tracing ...
sys.settrace(prev_tracefunc)

def my_tracefunc(...):
    ... do my stuff ...
    if prev_tracefunc is not None:
        return prev_tracefunc(...)
    return None
```

However, when running the code under `coverage` (either directly or using `nose`), I discovered that `prev_tracefunc` was still `None`; in addition, while my code run and passed its tests, coverage was not collected for the relevant parts - as per the documentation, but still surprising.

So:

* Is there any magic incantation I could place in my code which would allow it to live peacefully with `coverage.py`?

* If not, would it be possible to tweak `coverage.py` so that such an incantation would become possible?

issue_body:
I'm able to canonicalize an unbounded interval which somewhat changes the meaning of the interval.

As an example:
```
>>> a = IntInterval((None, None))
>>> infinity.inf in a
False
>>> b = canonicalize(a, lower_inc=True, upper_inc=True)
>>> b
IntInterval('[,]')
>>> infinity.inf in b
```

The initial interval suggests that infinity is not included in the interval, but after the canonicalization infinity is in the interval.

issue_title: Daemonize should kill the parent process more quickly
issue_body:
So Daemonize uses `sys.exit` in the parent process after the fork. The intention is likely that this will cause the parent process to immediately end.

However, this is not actually the case:
 * The parent python process raises SystemExit instead, which returns control flow outside of the Daemonize call if an exception handler for SystemExit is present. This means that code can continue to execute in the parent process after the Fork. Strange, but something a programmer can avoid with careful coding.
 * However, if the parent process has passed file descriptors of sockets/pipes/etc. to `keep_fds` that it intends for the client to use, when the parent process exits, the parent process will garbage collect many of these client objects and these garbage collection routines will then attempt to do clean-ups. These clean-ups may involve sending shutdown notices over the sockets/pipes. This corrupts the child process. We noticed this when we changed a client library to properly implement garbage collection.

I suggest that, instead of calling`sys.exit(0)`, that Daemonize reset the signal handlers to the system default handlers (away from the python default handlers that raise exceptions) and then call `os.kill` on itself with SIGTERM (or SIGKILL). This would prevent Python garbage collection routines in the parent from having the ability to interfere with the child process.

issue_title: Supporting multiple buttons in modules
issue_body:
(Opinion) For the multiple options to work fully in modules, we shouldn't limit buttons to a single number, but rather a list instead.

* Buttons (if int) is to be converted to list
* Buttons (if list) is to be left alone

And for all modules to be updated simply by replacing `==` with `in`.
```
./moc.py:153:        if button == self.button_pause:
./moc.py:158:        elif button == self.button_stop:
./moc.py:160:        elif button == self.button_next:
./moc.py:162:        elif button == self.button_previous:
```

to ...

```
./moc.py:153:        if button in self.button_pause:
./moc.py:158:        elif button in self.button_stop:
./moc.py:160:        elif button in self.button_next:
./moc.py:162:        elif button in self.button_previous:
```

Then the users would be able to do either `button_stop = 3` or `button_stop = [3, 7, 9]`.

It's a simple tweak that should have more "benefits" in the long run.

issue_title: Support for Existing Definitions and Signatures
issue_body:
I am very excited about a project like this finally making progress on proper concurrency support for grpc. 

I am curious about the vision for this library. I understand that grpcio is not a run time requirement, but since the standard grpcio library is already in wide use, I think it would be beneficial to create "shim" library instead of a replacement.

For example, consider using the grpc definitions generated by the upstream protobuf compiler and using the existing workflow and method signatures. This is made possible since a subclass can override a parent's method with an async version.

For example:
```python
# Standard gRPC

import futures
import grpc
import example_pb2
import example_pb2_grpc

class ExampleServicer(example_pb2_grpc.ExampleServicer):
    def GetSomething(request, context):
         value = do_something(request.param)
         return example_pb2.Something(value=value)

    def GetSomething2(request, context):
         value = do_something2(request.param)
         return example_pb2.Something2(value=value)

def serve():
    server = grpc.server(futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=10))
    example_pb2_grpc.add_ExampleServicer_to_server(ExampleServicer(), server)
    server.add_insecure_port('[::]:50051')
    server.start()
```
```python
# Shimmed gRPC

import asyncio
import grpclib
import example_pb2
import example_pb2_grpc

class ExampleServicer(example_pb2_grpc.ExampleServicer):
    async def GetSomething(request, context):
        # Notice this method has become async
        # and should be called as a coroutine.
        value = await do_something_async(request.param)
        return example_pb2.Something(value=value)

    def GetSomething2(request, context):
         # Notice this method is not async
         # and should be called as a normal method.
         # Perhaps run_in_executor?
         value = do_something2(request.param)
         return example_pb2.Something2(value=value)

def serve():
    loop = asyncio.get_event_loop()
    server = grpclib.server(loop)
    example_pb2_grpc.add_ExampleServicer_to_server(ExampleServicer(), server)
    server.add_insecure_port('[::]:50051')
    server.start()  # loop.run_forever() ?
```

I think this would massively increase the adoption rate of this library. We have a few hundred definitions at the moment and essentially rewriting the servicers would be difficult at this point. Being able to move over different rpc methods to async as we touch them would be amazing. Personally I could care less that the grpcio requirement is removed, since we are already using it at this point.

Another idea would be to make the behavior of grpclib match that of the orginal grpcio library. For example, we rely on exceptions being propagated in order to capture them by Sentry but grpclib behavior is to swallow exception. This means we would have to rely on some monkey patching to hook capturing of exceptions at this point.

Again, awesome work on this! I am just curious to see what other think about adopting a library like this in their projects.

:+1: :sunglasses:

issue_title: add feature checking as a verbose option to .predict and .predict_proba in FinalModelATC
issue_body:
Basically, we want the user to be able to ask "am i passing in all the right features?", and we'll tell them!

this will not be turned on by default. the user will have to pass in `.predict(data, verbose=True)` or something like that in order to get this logging when making predictions. 

Eventually, we can modify this to give them a value for "of the valuable features as judged by feature_importances_, what percent of them did you pass in?", but that'll be post_mvp. 

To get this working: MVP is to 

- [ ] get all the feature names (using a modified version of the functions we already have that get the feature_importances_, and we will maybe say this only works for tree-based methods that have feature_importances for now)
- [ ] set a property on FinalModelATC that holds all these new feature names (might need to make this settable in init?!)
- [ ] Inside predict and predict_proba, and inside the verbose flag
- [ ] set operations!
- [ ] set A not in set B (features you passed in that are not in our training set- clearly explained in the log)
- [ ] set B not in set A (features in the training set you did not pass in (clearly explained in the log)

over time we can add in more features:
handle categorical features intelligently (if you have one categorical_feature_name=cookies: True, that's good enough for all of those categorical features)
scoring (based on the feature_importances, what percent of the useful features did you pass in?)
avg values: save the avg value of each column in our training data (easy: do it as a dictionary), and then get the avg value of each column in our prediction set, and compare. present as a table: first column is feature_name, second column is training_avg, third column is prediction_avg, and fourth column is percent_delta. sort on the fourth column. or sort by feature importances. yeah, let's do that. fifth column is feature_importance, and we sort on that column.

issue_title: Non-standard bibtex field 'issue'
issue_body:
The correct BibTeX field for journal issue is confusingly called `number`, and `number` is used for other output types - e.g. series number for books, etc..

Before 025424757030062e7abda68a4566ce728aa1622e , BibTeX `number` was mapped to CSL `number`, but that was corrected to map to 'issue'.

When a record contains both an issue and a number, `bibutils` emits both.
If a record contains either an issue or a number, bibutils emits a `number`.

There is a comment explaining why in the `bibutils` source.
https://github.com/jayvdb/bibutils-archive/blame/master/lib/bibtexout.c#L508

```c
/*
 * from Tim Hicks:
 * I'm no expert on bibtex, but those who know more than I on our mailing 
 * list suggest that 'issue' isn't a recognised key for bibtex and 
 * therefore that bibutils should be aliasing IS to number at some point in 
 * the conversion.
 *
 * Therefore prefer outputting issue/number as number and only keep
 * a distinction if both issue and number are present for a particular
 * reference.
 */
```
static void
output_issue_number( FILE *fp, fields *info, int format_opts )
...

I suggest that citeproc-py follows suit.

issue_title: CEF Python running on Python 3.5/3.6 requires msvcp140.dll which apparently isn't shipped by Python
issue_body:
If you get such an error on Windows:
```Text
import cefpython3
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
  File "C:\Python3\lib\site-packages\cefpython3\__init__.py", line 59, in <module>
    from . import cefpython_py36 as cefpython
ImportError: DLL load failed: The specified module could not be found.
```

Then most probably this is caused, because you are missing "msvcp140.dll" dependency (for Python 3 for example). This is a dependency for Python C++ extensions (eg. cefpython_py36.pyd depends on it). For Python 3 to fix this download [Visual C++ Redistributable for VS2015](https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/download/details.aspx?id=48145) (13 MB) - this is just a VC++ redistributable, not a Visual Studio package. For 32-bit download "vc_redist.x86.exe" file and for 64-bit download "vc_redist.x64.exe" file.

Explanation: msvcp140.dll is the DLL for the C++ runtime library. This dependency is added by Cython when building CEF Python module. It seems that Python 3.6 only ships "vcruntime140.dll" which is the DLL for the C runtime library.

Rant: It would be nice if Guido van Rossum would stop his academic approach to Python development and would not have created Python 3 incompatibility hell. It would also be nice if he shipped msvcp140.dll along with vcruntime140.dll, so that Python 3 supports C++ extensions as well.

Created [Issue #359](../../issues/359) to track this problem.

On a side note, when using pyinstaller/py2exe tools for freezing application into exe then these tools should automatically detect the msvcp140.dll dependency and ship it with your application.

This info is also available in the Knowledge Base document.

## Possible solutions

1. Ship the msvcp140.dll along with other CEF and CEF Python binaries

issue_title: Remodel initialization
issue_body:
Could you consider remodelling initialization so it will follow two-phase model, like Flask-Security and a lot of other Flask extensions?

Here's an example of the pattern being used in application (FlaskBB):
[Phase 1 - Module where all extensions' objects are defined](https://github.com/sh4nks/flaskbb/blob/855bf9be73e482f6f6c4f621e9b8b14958c4bf79/flaskbb/extensions.py)
[Phase 2 - Initializing all the extensions in function called form app factory function](https://github.com/sh4nks/flaskbb/blob/855bf9be73e482f6f6c4f621e9b8b14958c4bf79/flaskbb/app.py#L88-L158)

AFAIK, it's currently impossible to do the two-phase initialization of Flask-User without making a circular import, because of object-creation-time dependencies:

- `flask_user.UserManager()` extension object (defined in extensions.py)
  - `flask_user.SQLAlchemyAdapter()` helper object (defined wherever)
    - `UserModel`  model class (defined in users blueprint)
      - `flask_sqlalchemy.SQLAlchemy()` extension object (defined in extensions.py)

And here's [how Flask-Security facilitates that](https://github.com/mattupstate/flask-security/blob/6b55e9613a93a34099124bfe3a3820dd902af9e1/flask_security/core.py#L395-L415).

Would you accept a pull request if I were to implement the change proposed here?

issue_title: ECC Point Compression
issue_body:
Currently the [`get_pubkey`](https://github.com/yann2192/pyelliptic/blob/master/pyelliptic/ecc.py#L116) and [`get_privkey`](https://github.com/yann2192/pyelliptic/blob/master/pyelliptic/ecc.py#L128) method returns public key and private key that is not compatible with other libraries like [SpongyCastle](https://github.com/rtyley/spongycastle) and [Cryptopp](https://github.com/mmoss/cryptopp).

As per the standards, we should use [Point Compression or Compact Point Representation](https://tools.ietf.org/html/draft-jivsov-ecc-compact-05).

issue_title: Breaks when all apt sources are FTP
issue_body:
Greetings,

Just tried a run on a freshly upgraded Ubuntu 14.10. After installing the `python3-bs4` package, I run the program directly, and receive the following output:

    #python3 ./apt-select.py -l
    Getting list of mirrors ... done.
    Testing 66 mirror(s) [66/66] 100%
    Traceback (most recent call last):
        File "./apt-select.py", line 277, in <module>
            repo_name = match(r'http://([\w\.\-]+)/', repo[0]).group(1)
     AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'group'

My python is quite weak, but it looks as if the tool is having a hard time parsing my sources.list file. I think this is because the regex on line 277 expects there to be at least one `http://` deb source, but [my file](https://gist.github.com/Karunamon/8f7731c762b18226ddf9) is all FTPs. If that line is changed as:

`repo_name = match(r'(http://|ftp://)([\w\.\-]+)/', repo[0]).group(1)`

..then all works as expected.

issue_title: insert_image using byte streams through "image_data" is not closing the io Object causing high memory consumption
issue_body:
I'm using worksheet.insert_image with the **image_data** parameter passing over a BytesIO object but this object is not getting closed after workbook.close() thus "leaking" memory.

When I make hundreds of insert_image calls on a worksheet, it keeps the BytesIO objects opened in memory after I close the workbook, also I can't for obvious reasons close the objects earlier otherwise it will cause an error (I/O operation on closed file).

By reading the source here: https://github.com/jmcnamara/XlsxWriter/blob/master/xlsxwriter/workbook.py#L1137-L1138

It seems you only close the file object only in cases when we insert an image from path (which you create a new file object with open).

Can't we change this line to something like:

```python
        if not image_data:
            fh.close()
        else:
            image_data.close()
```

so we close all BytesIO/StringIO file object streams "marking" them for GC?

On a side note, I have kept an reference to those BytesIO objects and called .close() on all of them after workbook.close() and this have severely reduced memory consumption on my use case (hundreds of images added to an worksheet).


refs #118

issue_title: Issue with image not resolving on download of Excel spreadsheet (using Django)
issue_body:
I am using XlsxWriter to create a spreadsheet in Django. The worksheets that have data are fine. However, when I download the spreadsheet, the image does not resolve. We tried both png and jpg as formats with the same problem.

Instead of the expected image, there is a red 'x' and a box that says, 'This image cannot currently be displayed' ([like so](https://i.stack.imgur.com/bR8VO.jpg)).

I have tried running a version of the script locally with the same image file. That works fine. 

I am using Python version 3.4 and XlsxWriter 0.9.6. This is with Excel 15.34 for Mac.

Here is the helper code that is putting the image into the spreadsheet:

```
    def add_image(self):
        '''
        Add the image to the worksheet
        '''
        sheet = self.book.add_worksheet('Image')
        for file in os.listdir(IMG_PATH):
            if self.file_name in file:
                try:
                    sheet.insert_image('A1', os.path.join(IMG_PATH, file))
                    break
                except Exception as e:
                    raise Exception("Problem with the image: {0}".format(e))
            else:
                sheet.write('A1', 'No Image file found')
```
The workbook is closed in another part of the class.

Here is the code in views.py:
```
def excel(request):
 job_name = request.GET.get('job_name')

    response = HttpResponse(content_type="application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet")
    response['Content-Disposition'] = "attachment; filename={0}.xlsx".format(job_name)
    return CreateExcel(response, job_name).response
```

Any help would be greatly appreciated!

issue_title: Password widget and validation
issue_body:
Hi there,

I have a problem creating a signup form with form validation for a field which is a password field **and** numbers-only field.
I have noticed that, if that field becomes valid, the validation message disappears, which does not happen for other fields.
Basically, the `ul` element with classes `djng-form-control-feedback djng-field-errors` does not contain the `li` element for `$valid` for password fields.
It seems to be that the incriminating piece of code (`angular_validation.py`) is the following:
```python
if not isinstance(bound_field.field.widget, widgets.PasswordInput):
            # all valid fields shall display OK tick after changed into dirty state
            errors.append(SafeTuple((identifier, self.field_error_css_classes, '$dirty', '$valid', 'valid', '')))
```

Would not be better to control this behavior by CSS?

issue_title: automatically dereference when only one key/value
issue_body:
Over in Postgres.py we [have](https://postgres-py.readthedocs.org/en/2.1.2/#tutorial) an API feature where we automatically dereference single-column queries. Do you want that feature, @kennethreitz? It would mean this:

```python
>>> db.query("select foo from bar").all()
[2, 4, 6, 8]
```

Instead of this:

```python
>>> db.query("select foo from bar").all()
[<Record "foo": 2>, <Record "foo": 4>, <Record "foo": 6>, <Record "foo": 8>]
```

issue_title: Handling http 301 in result['status']
issue_body:
Hi,
Many sites uses 3rd party software to distribute content (feedsportal, feedburner, etc.). In such case we always get 301 or 302  from http://mysite.com/feed to http://feeds.feedburner.com/mysite. In the end we get 200 or 304.

However `parse()` always return status set to 301. I think we should return final response code.
Here's my patch:
replace lines 
> 1969	    if hasattr(f, 'status'):
> 1970	        result['status'] = f.status

with 

> result['status'] = getattr(f, 'code', 0)

Or maybe we can add this as new entry in result (along with status)

issue_title: Possible performance improvement
issue_body: Consider changing ``db.UTXO`` from a namedtuple to a class using `__slots__` like [https://github.com/ofek/bit/blob/master/bit/network/meta.py#L6](https://github.com/ofek/bit/blob/master/bit/network/meta.py#L6). Instantiation is a tad quicker and it will save a byte or 2 of memory for each one.

issue_title: Breathe not generating any output for documented file
issue_body:
Hello!

I'm using Breathe/Doxygen/Sphinx to document a c++ project I'm a contributor on. Currently we have it all configured to generate documentation for a chunk of the files in our tools module, and it works perfectly on most of the files, but on some of them Breathe generates nothing or nearly nothing for the whole file.

An example:

This is a snapshot of the HTML docs; as you can see, there's docs for graph_utils.h and for macros.h, but nothing for grid.h
![image](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/2142082/15788119/d48ffbdc-2993-11e6-83a2-6c0ac5b8063a.png)

Here's the top of grid.h:

```
  #include <vector>                                                               
                                                                                  
  #include "assert.h"                                                             
  #include "BitVector.h"                                                          
                                                                                  
  namespace emp {                                                                 
  namespace Grid {                                                                
                                                                                  
    template <typename CELL_TYPE> class Cell;                                     
    template <typename EDGE_TYPE> class VEdge;                                    
    template <typename EDGE_TYPE> class HEdge;                                    
    template <typename POINT_TYPE> class Point;                                   
                                                                                  
    class Layout {                                                                
    private:                                                                      
      int width;                                                                  
      int height;                                                                 
      std::vector< std::vector<int> > regions;                                    
    public:                                                                       
      Layout(int w, int h) : width(w), height(h) { ; }                            
      Layout(const Layout &) = default;                                           
      ~Layout() { ; }                                                             
      Layout & operator=(const Layout &) = default;                               
                                                                                  
      int GetWidth() const { return width; }                                      
      int GetHeight() const { return height; }                                    
      int GetNumRegions() const { return (int) regions.size(); }                  
      const std::vector<int> & GetRegion(int id) { return regions[id]; }
```

I don't see anything obvious that would be causing this to break.

We are using doxygen 1.8.11, sphinx 1.4.1 and breathe from the master branch.

To reproduce:

```
# get empirical
git clone https://github.com/devosoft/Empirical.git
cd Empirical
# assuming you have flex, bison etc., install the dependencies for the project:

source third-party/env/bin/activate

Any help would be appreciated!

issue_title: Test failure in `objects/mutator.py`
issue_body:
On Python 3 there is a test failure in mutator.py, because the order of the axis names being returned is different if the same test is run on Python 2. Mutator.getAxisNames() is creating a list of axis names like this:

```python
        s = {}
        for l, x in self.items():
            s.update(dict.fromkeys([k for k, v in l], None))
        return list(s.keys())
```

In other words, the test is relying on the order given by list() on the unordered set of dictionary keys. Given that this list of axis names is later used only by Location.expand(), and that each item of the list is used as keys on a dictionary, which is a set… they should probably just be a set from the beginning. 

Original Traceback below:

```python
**********************************************************************
File "Lib/mutatorMath/objects/mutator.py", line 589, in __main__.test_methods
Failed example:
    m.getAxisNames()
Expected:
    ['snap', 'pop']
Got:
    ['pop', 'snap']
**********************************************************************
1 items had failures:
   1 of   2 in __main__.test_methods
***Test Failed*** 1 failures.
```

Again, I have a fix on hand for this issue, pull request following shortly.

issue_title: Documentation
next_run_time set to next round hour produces error
issue_body:
I'm running a basic BlockingScheduler everyday at a given hour by passing `next_run_time` when I add the job. If I start the scheduler before the given time, that's good, it will run at the given time. I I start it after the given time, I make it run the next day at the given time. 
That way I can deploy (and thus start the scheduler) whenever I want to. It works well except for one case. 
Let's say the given time is noon (it's all UTC, so no errors there). 
If I start the scheduler at 10:xx, no problems, the job is run at noon the same day.
If I start the scheduler at 13:xx, no problems, the job is run at noon the next day. 

However, when I start the scheduler at 11:xx (hour just before the set time), I get an error:
`No handlers could be found for logger "apscheduler.executors.default"`

I know I get this error when I set `next_run_time` in the past, but that is not the case here.

My script:
```
next_run_time = datetime.utcnow().replace(hour=12, minute=0, second=0, microsecond=0)
if next_run_time < datetime.utcnow():
    one_day = timedelta(days=1)
    next_run_time = next_run_time + one_day
print datetime.utcnow()
print next_run_time
scheduler = BlockingScheduler()
scheduler.add_job(run_script, 'interval', next_run_time=next_run_time, days=int(os.environ['DAYS_SPAN']))
try:
    scheduler.start()
except (KeyboardInterrupt, SystemExit):
    pass
```
An example, when it's 20:38 UTC, 2017-11-14 and I set hour=21. I print the current UTC time and `next_run_time`. This is the console output:
```
2017-11-14 20:38:17.765191
2017-11-14 21:00:00
No handlers could be found for logger "apscheduler.executors.default"
```

Am I missing something or is this a bug?

issue_title: Library can report incorrect error messages
issue_body:
Consider the following schema:

```python
s = Schema(
    Any(
        {Required('field'): In({'field_a', 'field_b'}), Required('value'): str},
        {Required('field'): In({'field_c', 'field_d'}), Required('value'): int},
    )
)
```

If I call `s({'field': 'field_a'})` I get:

- `voluptuous.MultipleInvalid: required key not provided @ data['value']`

If I call `s({'field': 'field_c'})` I get:

- `voluptuous.MultipleInvalid: value is not allowed for dictionary value @ data['field']`

The first of these makes sense, the second doesn't because `'field_c'` is a valid value for `'field'` in at least one alternative in `Any`.

This may be tricky to solve in the general case. When calling with ``s({'field': 'field_c'})` there are two errors for the first option (invalid "field" value and missing "value" field) and only one error for the second option (missing "value" field). So it may be possible to present the error message associated with the "least bad" match.

Hi

I have a long running process that subscribes to the queue once and then keeps listening to it.

The problem is: if there is nothing pushed to the queue for a long time(~12 hours in my case), the connection still remains active(I can see it in the ActiveMQ web console) but when I start pushing data to the queue again(after 12 hours(say)), on_message does not get invoked.

However, the ActiveMQ web console shows that messages are being dequeued.

 I have to kill the process and start it again and everything works just fine.

issue_title: setuptools-specific features in setup.py break Travis build
issue_body:
In the latest release branch, I converted `setup.py` to use the proper notation for supported Python versions (ccf48c30) and platform-specific dependencies (47203ee3). Unfortunately, that [broke the Travis build](https://travis-ci.org/jgonggrijp/pip-review/builds/220136344). For some unclear reason that I need help with, these features cause the installation step to fail at `pip install .`. I do not see these errors if I run the tests locally with `tox`.

I tried updating pip (a29db746), but that [didn't help](https://travis-ci.org/jgonggrijp/pip-review/builds/220140481). I also tried ensuring that setuptools is installed (6e428696), but that [didn't make a difference](https://travis-ci.org/jgonggrijp/pip-review/builds/220142953), either. I decided to accept the broken Travis builds for now and release anyway (0.5.3), because I felt reasonably certain that `pip-review` still installs and works, but the problem is unsolved.

If somebody knows how to solve this, help will be much appreciated.

issue_title: Remove Googlebot from user agent check to prevent cloaking penalty
issue_body:
https://github.com/skoczen/django-seo-js/blob/master/django_seo_js/settings.py#L49

Googlebot, Yahoo, bingbot should be removed from that list as soon as possible to prevent anyone from being penalized for cloaking. Those three crawlers support the escaped fragment protocol so that you do not have to match them by user agent. If you do match them by user agent, they could think you are cloaking and penalize your website in the search results.

issue_title: Modification time is not logarithmic
issue_body:
I created a progress bar so that I could see some feedback on the long-running issue4 test (konstantint/PyIntervalTree#4). I found that the time to modify tree is increasing roughly linearly with the size of the tree. This makes the total creation time quadratic, and the time-size plot is looking like this:

    y(x) = (10^3*x)^2 * 3.7*10^-8

![actual](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/7785285/5407068/7f6d3bd4-8173-11e4-84ee-f52e74abb015.png)
Legend: total creation time (s) vs. number of operations on tree (x1000)

Instead, the time to insert or remove an Interval should increase logarithmically, and the total creation time should be the integral of that, which is x log x - x. With a rough scale correction, the plot should look more like this:

    y(x) = 1.42*10^-5 * (10^3*x * ln(10^3*x) - 10^3*x)

![ideal](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/7785285/5407011/892fcd8c-8171-11e4-8d51-d7eacd25c9c6.png)
Legend: total creation time (s) vs. number of operations on tree (x1000)

issue_title: Feature request: support `queue_derive` in `modify_metadata`
issue_body:
Here's my use case: I have an item to upload with multiple files and metadata that varies per file. So I'm calling `upload(id, files=files, metadata=metadata)` once with the item metadata, then repeatedly calling `modify_metadata(id, target="files/<filename>", metadata=<file_metadata>)` for each of the files. I'd like to not start a derive until the entire process is finished. I can set `queue_derive=False` on the `upload` call, but `modify_metadata` doesn't take a `queue_derive` or `headers` argument, so there's no obvious way to start the derive task short of re-uploading one of the files (e.g., the smallest one).

I would also be happy if someone can suggest some no-op operation that would allow me to queue a derive as a side effect.

issue_title: Apparently repeatable 'Broken Pipe' error on --spreadsheet= upload
issue_body:
This might be the same issue as #176. If so I'll happily delete this one and attach this text to that issue.

Each weekend I prepare a CSV file of 5 rows (6 with the header) relating to the previous week's podcast episodes (Hacker Public Radio, one episode every weekday - sorry for the plug!). Today's batch was for episodes 2311-2315.

Running `ia upload --spreadsheet=` resulted in a 'Broken Pipe' failure after the first row was processed. I have a copy of the error, but it looks pretty much identical to the example in #176 so I'll omit it here. It's available on request.

I retried the upload between 5 and 6 times throughout the day, all with the same result.

I decided to try dividing the CSV file, making two: 2311-2312 and 2313-2315. Trying 2311-2312 failed on 2311. File 2313-2315 was completely successful.

I retried 2311-2312 twice, then edited the file to reverse the order of the rows. Uploading this I saw 2312 uploaded successfully but the upload failed on 2311 with a 'Broken Pipe' error.

Testing the hypothesis that 2311 was somehow faulty I generated a new CSV file containing only that episode and tried to upload it. It succeeded.

I have seen a similar scenario before. If my 5-row upload fails, it invariably fails on the first row. Generating two files containing 1 row and 4 rows then results in success in both cases. I have kept a brief log of these events since 2017-01-07.

It's possible my CSV-generating script is doing something odd, but it's difficult to see what! You're welcome to have copies of these particular files if it'll help.

I hope this adds something useful to the discussion.

issue_title: slow render performance with custom pagination and large datasets
issue_body:
angular version: tested with **1.2.x, 1.4.x, and 1.5.x**
smart table version: **2.1.8**

table renders slowly with large data set and custom pagination template.  only happens if the data is already loaded or cached.  if the page loads and then data is loaded asynchronously, or if you use $timeout with even 100ms delay, this is not an issue

i think this happens because, when using a custom pagination template, there seems to be some delay in smart-table recognizing pagination is enabled... 

line 176 `displaySetter($scope, output || filtered);`

when the table is rendered, `output` (paginated output) does not exist yet so it is using `filtered`.  you can see this as the table loads because the the table tries to load the entire recordset in to the DOM before correctly slicing the array and showing just the paged results

a hack i used to fix this issue is `displaySetter($scope, output);` which is obviously not a real fix since it always assumes pagination.  

with that hack
https://plnkr.co/edit/XGh7vLcynwWxtZnhXy5L?p=info
for me, fully runs/loads in ~3 seconds from clicking "run"

without that hack:
https://plnkr.co/edit/4UYHOuzwJFhWKKozXfYl?p=info
for me, fully runs/loads in ~7 seconds from clicking "run". more than double the time as with the hack

there is probably a much better solution, but for now if anyone else is running in to this issue, you could add a pagination boolean to the top level smart-table directive and then line 176 would become something like

`ctrl.paginateTrue ? displaySetter($scope, output) : displaySetter($scope, filtered);`

or if you don't want to mess with the smart-table code, just use a $timeout to add a small delay which also seems to fix the render issue

instead of `$scope.rowCollection = someService.cachedData;`

`$timeout(function(){
  $scope.rowCollection = someService.cachedData;
},100);`

issue_title: Implicit continuations with f-strings cause SyntaxErrors
issue_body:
Line continuations that contain f-strings aren't properly transpiled.

Test script:
```python
# -*- coding: future_fstrings -*-
a = 1
b = 2

# Escaped newline
c = f"a={a} " \
    f"b={b}"
print(c)

# Implied line continuation inside parentheses
print(f"a={a} "
      f"b={b}")
```

Output with Python 3.6:
```
a=1 b=2
a=1 b=2
```

Error with Python 3.5:
```
  File "asd.py", line 6
    "b={}".format((b))
         ^
SyntaxError: invalid syntax
```

Code:

from exchangelib import DELEGATE, Account, Credentials, Configuration, NTLM
creds = Credentials(

account = Account(

issue_title: Revision not showing in admin when creating a revision for a followed relationship with the decorator
issue_body:
Hi! First of all thanks for that awesome library.

*Note: tested with v1 and v2*

I have a model A and a model B with a foreign key relationship.

```python
@reversion.register(follow=['b'])
class A(models.Model):
    pass

@reversion.register()
class B(models.Model):
    a = models.ForeignKey(A)
```

When modifying an inlined instance of `B` in `A` ModelAdmin from the admin panel, I can see the revision in `A` instance admin page history tab.

When creating a revision with `@reversion.create_revision()` decorator and update an instance of `B`, the revision is not showing in the `A` instance admin page history tab.  

I tried to see what revisions are created from the shell when I use the decorator and it seems the revision for the `B` instance is created but not the revision for `A`, hence why it is not showing in the admin page.

If I do the modification from the admin page, the revision for `A` is created as it should.

Am I forgetting something?

issue_title: revisionform_view throws RevertError when there's a unique constraint
issue_body:
I have a unique constraint on a couple of fields of a model. I can see the "Recover deleted" list but when I click on a deleted object that - if it were to be recovered - would violate the unique constraint, then safe_revert() throws RevertError("Could not revert revision, due to database integrity errors.").

I would have hoped to see the deleted object in a form, and then get the unique constraint error when trying to save, but this seems to be happening one step earlier. It's confusing for end users, and it also means the string representation of the deleted objects in the recover list needs to show _all_ relevant information, as you won't get to see more details.

issue_title: Support multiple folders/files/classes on the cmd line
Django 2.1 stopped supporting widgets without explicit renderer arg definition
issue_body:
As we can see in the release notes for Django 2.1 (https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/2.1/releases/2.1/):
> Support for Widget.render() methods without the renderer argument is removed.

As the ColorFieldWidget render method does not have a `renderer` kwarg, it fails when rendering:
```python
def render(self, name, value, attrs={}):
```
It should be enough to just add:
```python
def render(self, name, value, attrs={}, **kwargs):
```

    name = models.CharField(max_length=256, blank=False, null=False)


class B(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=256, blank=False, null=False)
    a = models.ForeignKey('A',
                          models.DO_NOTHING,
                          blank=True,
                          null=True,
                          related_name='b_rel')
    content_type = models.ForeignKey(ContentType, models.DO_NOTHING)
    object_id = models.PositiveIntegerField()
    content_object = GenericForeignKey('content_type', 'object_id')
```

If you try to filter the model 'A' list by the 'b_rel' field the error appears.

Since i actually tried filtering trough djangorestframework 3.3.3 (even if i think that thi issue is generic), i will leave here the relevant code to publish model 'A' to a rest API so you can test it easily.
Just create an 'A' record and paste the following url after you authenticated (trough the django rest framework browsable api or standard django admin):
`http://localhost:8000/api/your-app-name/a/?b_rel=1`

**views.py**

```
from .serializers import *
from projects.models import A, B
from rest_framework.viewsets import ModelViewSet


class ASerializer(serializers.ModelSerializer):
    class Meta:
        model = A
        fields = [
            'name',
            'b_rel',
        ]


class AViewSet(ModelViewSet):
    serializer_class = ASerializer
    queryset = A.objects.all()
    filter_backends = [DjangoFilterBackend]
    filter_fields = [
        'name',
        'b_rel',
    ]
```

**urls.py**

```
from django.conf.urls import include, url
from rest_framework import routers
from projects.views import AViewSet

router = routers.DefaultRouter()

router.register(r'a', AViewSet, 'projects-a')

urlpatterns = [
    url(r'^api/your-app-name/', include(router.urls)),
]
```

Since the error occurs when filters for the related model ('B') are built automatically, imho the solution is to check wheter a field actually has a formfield or at least exclude the 'GenericForeignKey' field from filters because they do not have a form field by default, see [relevant django documentation.](https://docs.djangoproject.com/ja/1.9/ref/contrib/contenttypes/#generic-relations).

If you prefer a pull request, just tell me.

I'm getting this several times a day:

TeleBot: Exception occurred. Stopping.
getUpdates failed. Returned result: <Response [502]>

Knowing that '*The 502 Bad Gateway error is an HTTP status code that means that one server received an invalid response from another server*', I believe it is an error on Telegram's server, not on the API. 

So I would be happy if someone could give me an suggestion on how to treat this and make my bot automatically begin to work again.

issue_title: High number of Thread in log
issue_body:
_1. What version of pyTelegramBotAPI are you using?_
3.6.3 + last commits
_2. What OS are you using?_
Ubuntu 16.04.4
_3. What version of python are you using?_
3.5.2

I looked at the log file after a week of bot online and see:

> 2018-06-13 21:18:19,294 (__init__.py:194 Thread-672) DEBUG - TeleBot: "Received 1 new updates"
> 2018-06-13 22:01:19,229 (__init__.py:194 Thread-673) DEBUG - TeleBot: "Received 1 new updates"
> 2018-06-13 22:01:21,239 (__init__.py:194 Thread-674) DEBUG - TeleBot: "Received 1 new updates"
 
The number of thread is increased every time the bot receive an update.
Workers numbers are fine:

> 2018-06-13 13:23:02,194 (__init__.py:194 Thread-560) DEBUG - TeleBot: "Received 1 new updates"
> 2018-06-13 13:23:02,195 (util.py:56 WorkerThread1) DEBUG - TeleBot: "Received task"

I'm confused of thread numbers: do the bot really had 674 active threads?
Or such a thread is created and then killed and this is just the counter increase?

**PS.** Code inspection of logger module shows that it uses Thread.name:
```
        if logThreads and threading:
            self.thread = threading.get_ident()
            self.threadName = threading.current_thread().name
```
Pyhton docs say:

> name is the thread name. By default, a unique name is constructed of the form “Thread-N” where N is a small decimal number.

But all this still gives me no answer to my question. :(

issue_body:
I want to test two dataframes using DataFrameSuiteBase but it appears that SparkSession object is null all the time and never gets created while tests are running?

```
package com.ta.geo.calculation

import com.holdenkarau.spark.testing.DataFrameSuiteBase
import com.ta.geo.calculation.GeodistanceCalculation.matchTrackingEventsWithNearestAirport
import com.ta.geo.datasource.ModelDatasource
import com.ta.geo.measure.{DistanceMeasure, HaversineDistance}

import org.apache.spark.sql.{Dataset, SparkSession}
import org.scalatest.{FlatSpec, Matchers}

class GeodistanceCalculationTest extends FlatSpec with Matchers with DataFrameSuiteBase {

  override implicit def reuseContextIfPossible: Boolean = true

  implicit val sqlCtx: SparkSession = spark
  import sqlCtx.implicits._

  val ds: ModelDatasource = new ModelDatasource // uses sparksession to read dataset
  val airports: Dataset[Airport] = ds.getAirports("data/optd-sample-20161201.csv")

  "GeolocationCalculation" should "return Berlin Tempelhof Airport (THF)." in {
    val events = Seq(TrackingEvent("TravelAudience", 52.4897337, 13.4554343)).toDS()

    val actual = matchTrackingEventsWithNearestAirport(events, airports, new HaversineDistance)
    val expected = Seq(("TravelAudience", "THF")).toDF("uuid", "iata_code")

    assertDataFrameEquals(actual, expected)
  }
  
}
```

issue_title: Only use "mention" if
Remove Markdown rendering
issue_body: While it might sometimes come in handy to *render an element as Markdown* I would prefer it if `requests-html` would stay lightweight and focused on one clear problem without throwing in too many nice-to-have stuff .

issue_title: Would you consider supporting local HTML files?
issue_body:
One of my uses of BeautifulSoup is parsing a file that takes a few minutes to get generated on the server, so I tend to download it and work on it locally, something like:

```
with open('report-filtered.html') as html_file:
    soup = BeautifulSoup(html_file, 'html.parser')
```

From a quick look at the `HTML` class this would be quite straightforward to support, we'd set `self.html` to the content of that file, and `self.url` would be None. The result of that is that `absolute_links` wouldn't work (unless URL was passed in too), and perhaps the `__repr__` would be more useful with knowledge of a filename too. Maybe the best way would be self `self.url` to the filename and feature-gate making links absolute? Obviously there's a few things to think about implementation wise, but I'd be happy to put the PR together if there's interest. I wanted to check first though whether that's something you'd consider/be interested in! :)

issue_title: mail_context_processor
issue_body:
Could someone provide an example of implementing ```mail_context_processor()```?

I'm using  the application factory pattern for setup, i.e. ```init_app```

I've tried accessing the extension as below (per #211 and #340), but am receiving
```python
 RuntimeError: working outside of application context
```
```python
from flask import current_app

security_ctx = current_app.extensions['security']

@security_ctx.mail_context_processor()
def security_mail_processor():
        return dict(hello="world")
```

I have limited understanding of context processors, I'm not sure how to resolve.

issue_title: Namespaces should be lazy registerable
issue_body:
As @frol suggested, for big APIs splitted on multiple files, Namespace should be the only class to import.
As Blueprints for Flask, it should provide all necessary methods to not require the API instance in the file and to be lazy registerable.

The target is being able to write:

```python
# feature.py
from flask_restplus import Namespace, Resource

ns = Namespace('feature', 'Some namespace')

model = ns.model('Model', {})

@ns.route('/')
class MyFeature(Resource):
    @ns.marshal_with(model)
    def get(self):
        pass      
```

```python
# api.py
from flask_restplus import Api
from feature import ns

api = Api()
api.register_namespace(ns)
```
