issue_title: Use cases: batch processing and resource consumption
issue_body:
Can we simulate the given use case using ciw:
1) Say, there is a bicycle rental station where there are 100 cycles present at a time. Now the customers arrive with a certain distribution to take the cycle. Each customer is allowed one cycle at a time. The new batch of cycles comes only after 60 minutes which means that the empty cycle servers are refilled only after 60 minutes. A customer baulks if there are no cycles currently present and the refilling of the cycles will take  more than 30 minutes. The rented cycles are returned to the warehouse. There amount of resources(cycles) available in the warehouse can be taken as any number. Is there a way this can be simulated?

2) In a metro station, or even production lines, a batch of customers are served simultaneously. Like 100 people enter the metro together. Is there a method for batch processing available?

issue_title: CanvasAPI Release v0.8.1
issue_body: Several bugfixes and documentation fixes that need to go in ASAP.

issue_title: OutcomeGroup from list of subgroups does not have appropriate context information
issue_body:
While traversing a tree of OutcomeGroups, it can be handy to do the following:

```python
root_account = canvas.get_account(1)
root_group = root_account.get_root_outcome_group()
subgroup = root_group.get_subgroups()[0]
```

This pattern gives you the first (apparent) outcome group on your account. However, `subgroup` is not nearly as useful as it should be:

```python
subgroup.get_linked_outcomes()[0]
```
```
  File ".\2-outcome-group-fun.py", line 29, in <module>
    subgroup.get_linked_outcomes()[0]
  File "[...]\canvasapi\canvasapi\outcome.py", line 205, in get_linked_outcomes
    '{}/outcome_groups/{}/outcomes'.format(self.context_ref(), self.id),
  File "[...]\canvasapi\canvasapi\outcome.py", line 103, in context_ref
    if self.context_type == 'Course':
AttributeError: 'OutcomeGroup' object has no attribute 'context_type'
```

It appears that this is because the API endpoint `/api/v1/accounts/:id/outcome_groups/:id/subgroups` returns an abbreviated form of the OutcomeGroup object. It doesn't have a `context_type`!

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/21966173/42650397-ea19f63c-85d1-11e8-956a-6b7193822264.png)

In theory, this has all the data we need to continue our traversal by using the `subgroups_url` or `outcomes_url`, but CanvasAPI currently tries to use the `context_type` value to build its own URL for `get_subgroups()` and `get_linked_outcomes()`.

### Workaround

It is possible to toss an OutcomeGroup back to the `Account.get_outcome_group()` method to get the full OutcomeGroup. Continuing our previous example...

```python
subgroup = root_account.get_outcome_group(subgroup)
subgroup.get_linked_outcomes()[0]
```
```
OutcomeLink(_requester=<canvasapi.requester.Requester object at 0x03801230>, context_id=1, context_type=Account [...] )
```

issue_title: Awkward to set context_codes[] parameter in Calendar.list_calendar_events call
issue_body:
The `Canvas.list_calendar_events` method feeds its parameters down to a `GET /api/v1/calendar_events` request. [That request](https://canvas.instructure.com/doc/api/calendar_events.html#method.calendar_events_api.index) accepts a `context_codes[]` parameter, but setting that parameter correctly is surprisingly awkward in version 0.6.0 of `canvasapi`. The problem is that the parameter name really must be `context_codes[]`, not `context_codes`, but creating a Python keyword argument with brackets in its name is subtle:

```python
context = 'course_123456'

canvas.list_calendar_events(context_codes=context) 
# canvasapi.exceptions.CanvasException: API encountered an error processing your request

canvas.list_calendar_events(context_codes=[context])
# canvasapi.exceptions.CanvasException: API encountered an error processing your request

canvas.list_calendar_events(context_codes[]=context)
# SyntaxError: invalid syntax

canvas.list_calendar_events('context_codes[]'=context)
# SyntaxError: invalid syntax

canvas.list_calendar_events(**{'context_codes[]': context})
# success!
```

The syntactically invalid ones I knew were wrong before I even tried them. But for the first two attempts shown above, I guess I expected `canvasapi` to add the brackets for me either because it knew that `context_codes[]` was special, or perhaps it would always append brackets when I passed a value as a list (`[context]`) rather than a single item.

Arguably this is my fault: give the wrong parameter name, and of course things will fail. But I would have hoped that `canvasapi` would make this easier. The use of `**{...}` is obscure enough that many `canvasapi` users may not know how to do it at all.

issue_title: Odd copyright in LICENSE
issue_body:
https://github.com/SOBotics/Botpy/blob/master/LICENSE#L4 says

> Copyright (C) 2018 Ashish Ahuja (Fortunate-MAN) <ashish.ahuja@sobotics.org> 

My reading of https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/WTFPL suggests that this is a copyright statement _for the license itself_ and that it should probably say it's (C) 2004 Sam Hocevar <sam@hocevar.net>

The instructions at http://www.wtfpl.net/faq/ vaguely supports this interpretation; in particular, the color-coded explanation of the license terms highlights section 2 as "the rules for the license" and includes the copyright statement.

issue_title: Uid not set on Azure AD authentication and NoneType error
issue_body:
I'm trying to setup social-core, social-core-django, and Azure AD backend to authenticate using Azure Active Directory. The initial authentication works and creates a User and User Social Auth. However, subsequent attempts to authorize result in:

- A new User object (but no corresponding User Social Auth object)
- The server error 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'provider' raised at social_core/actions.py in do_complete, line 69

Some relevant information:
- The Uid field in the User Social Auth object created from the first Authorization is `None`
- The Azure AD is set up to include the "Sign in and read user profile" delegated permission (which corresponds to the `User.Read` permission)
- [Reading the backend code, it looks like the email address and ID is supposed to come from a field 'upn' corresponding to the user's on-premises user principal name (UPN)](https://github.com/python-social-auth/social-core/blob/master/social_core/backends/azuread.py#L63). The User Name field as listed in Azure AD
- The id_token JWT contains the following fields: aud, iss, iat, nbf, exp, aio, amr, email, family_name, given_name, idp, ipaddr, name, oid, platf, sub, tid, unique_name, uti, and ver. Note that the JWT does not containt the upn.
- The extra data stored with the User Social Auth includes auth_time, access_token, id_token, refresh_token, expires, expires_on, not_before (null), first_name, last_name, token_type, and resource (null)

What I've tried so far:
- I've tried using both the default pipeline, and the pipeline described in the documentation at https://python-social-auth-docs.readthedocs.io/en/latest/configuration/django.html#personalized-configuration 

Key questions:
- How is the Azure AD server supposed to pass the `upn` back to the client? Is it in the JWT?
- Is the missing UPN also the root cause for the NoneType server error on subsequent authentications?

I'm going to follow up with Microsoft Azure AD support to understand why the UPN is not being returned given my configuration, and will include their response

issue_title: Allow ray.init to discover that one instance of Ray is already running

issue_title: Creating actor and calling actor method immediately leads to hanging.
issue_body:
The following script currently hangs.

```python
import ray

ray.init()

@ray.remote
class Foo(object):
    def __init__(self):
        pass

    def bar(self):
        pass

ray.get(Foo.remote().bar.remote())
```

The reason is the following. There are three tasks invoked on this actor, `__init__`, `__ray_terminate__`, and the `bar`. And they get invoked in that order. That is, the actor handle destructor runs before the call to `bar`, so the `bar` method gets invoked after the actor has already been killed.

This suggests that the method invocation should have a reference to the actor handle so that the actor handle is still in scope while the method is being invoked.

issue_title: Debug lines starting with certain comment
Damaged session if direct_tcpip channel open fails
issue_body:
```
import socket
from ssh2.session import Session
from ssh2.listener import *

username='user'
passwd='password'
serverA='myServerA'
serverB='doesNotExist'

#open socket connection
sock = socket.socket(socket.IF_INET, socket.SOCK_STREAM)
sock.connect((serverA, 22))

#open session and authenticate
session = Session()
session.handshake(sock)
session.userauth_password(username, passwd)
session.set_timeout(2000)

#demonstrate successful channel open and execute command on serverA
chan = session.open_session()
if chan:
    print('successfully opened channel with serverA')
    chan.execute('ls')
    chan.close()
else:
    print('Failed to open channel with serverA')

#Try to open direct tcpip channel, via serverA SSH tunnel, to unreachable serverB
direct_chan = session.direct_tcpip(serverB, 22)
if direct_chan:
    print('ServerB was reached')
else:
    print('ServerB was unreachable')

#Cannot now open channel with serverA - why???
chan = session.open_session()
if chan:
    print('successfully opened channel with serverA')
    chan.execute('ls')
    chan.close()
else:
    print('Failed to open channel with serverA')

session.disconnect()
sock.close()
```

__Expected behaviour:__ 
Assume serverA is a reachable SSH server, and serverB is unreachable.  You successfully open a channel and execute a command on serverA.  With the same session, you try to open a tunneled direct_tcpip connection to an unreachable serverB.  The direct_tcpip channel fails as expected.  I would then still expect to be able to use the same session to sucessfully open a channel and execute a command  on serverA a second time

__Actual behaviour:__ 
The second time you try to open a channel to serverA to execute a command, the open_session fails.  It's like the session has been damaged by the failure to open the direct_tcpip connection to serverB. 

Note that if serverB is reachable, then the session is still ok, and you can still open a channel to serverA and execute a command.

Why does this happen and is there a fix?  I want to continue using a session for communication with serverA even if serverB is unreachable.

( Not sure if my SSH session/channel terminology is exactly correct, but hopefully my meaning is clear from the code )

issue_title: PressureFunctionDiagnosticComponent might need surface pressure
issue_body:
`PressureFunctionDiagnosticComponent` is used for certain initialisations in climt. It is used to initialise longwave optical depth, but that function needs (rather should use) sigma values rather than pressure values.

Consequently, the values of longwave optical depth in the default state are off by 5 orders of magnitude.

I propose this component accept surface pressure by default, so that any such calculation can be
performed easily.

issue_title: [V3 Config] Suggestions
issue_body:
I have two suggestions for feature enhancements for Config.  The first seems pretty core to me while the second is definitely a stretch type goal.

1)  Add `__contains__` to Value class, and hence Group, to allow `key in cfg`.  This is especially helpful for settings type Config where an end user is allowed to change settings that are stored in Config.  It would allow easy testing for data validation as one example.   If necessary for it to be async, I think `await cfg.contains(key)` type construct would be acceptable too.

2)  In the spirit of data validation, allow values to be registered as acceptable inputs.  For example min/max values for numeric inputs, or a set to be defined for a choices type Config (e.g. {'sci', 'eng', 'prefix'} as acceptable choices for a "format" type config).  This would allow streamlined code for a good subset of use cases.

issue_title: Auto-resolve addresses in socket methods
issue_body:
Currently, one of the awkward differences between `socket` and `trio.socket` – really the last awkward difference as of v0.2.0 – is that with `socket`, you can pass hostnames directly to socket methods, like `sock.bind(("localhost", 0))` or `sock.connect(("python.org", 443))`. In Trio these methods currently require numeric addresses, and there are the extra methods `resolve_local_address` and `resolve_remote_address` that do the resolution for you.

This was always a little weird, and really the only compelling reason was because `bind` as synchronous. But it turns out that `bind` should be async after all (#241). So we might as well get rid of this last piece of weirdness.

Plan:

* Make `bind`, `connect`, etc. all call `resolve_local_address`/`resolve_remote_address` automatically as needed
* Deprecate `resolve_local_address`/`resolve_remote_address`

issue_title: MX shape not behaving like typical Python/NumPy arrays
issue_body:
See also #64 

Most important being that higher order arrays cannot be handled at all. We could hypothetically wrap MX ourselves, but if we also want to return array/vector expressions to users, this is ugly as hell. It would then be much better to let CasADi support >2D. If however, we only ever want to return scalar expressions, we could go that way.

As far as ambiguous `Real[3]` times `Real[3]` is concerned, Modelica handles that as follows (see http://book.xogeny.com/behavior/arrays/functions/ ):

1. Normal multiplication operator behaves as dot product.
2. To get 3x3 matrix as result, use `outerProduct(a, b)` function.

Examples of the code where we workaround limitations of the "everything is a 2D matrix" approach:

- https://github.com/pymoca/pymoca/blob/87a97cb504a0c3fd7b313ed7003c33f7f8b12a81/src/pymoca/backends/casadi/generator.py#L330
  Tranpose check. In Modelica, one typically defined arrays/vectors as 
  ```Modelica
  Real x[3];
  ```
  This could end up as either (3,1) or (1,3) in MX (depending on intermediary operations). This check is there to transpose when there's a discrepancy between the LHS and RHS.

- https://github.com/pymoca/pymoca/blob/de50f66ce80417a5dcc2c335533af038e0907cec/src/pymoca/backends/casadi/model.py#L41
  Variable stores shape based on CasADi information, and is also reinitialized based on that when caching is used. 

- https://github.com/pymoca/pymoca/blob/de50f66ce80417a5dcc2c335533af038e0907cec/src/pymoca/backends/casadi/model.py#L333
  Vectors of size one (numpy style `shape = (1,)`), or e.g matrices of shape (1,1)), or scalars are all just assumed to be scalars. Either we would have to add aliases (x, x[1], x[1,1], x[1,1,1], x[1,1,1,1], ...), or do it right by only adding the symbols that users expect to exist. I.e. 
  ```Modelica
  Real x[1];
  ```
  leads to a symbol named `x[1]`, and not `x` (as is currently the case).
- https://github.com/pymoca/pymoca/blob/de50f66ce80417a5dcc2c335533af038e0907cec/src/pymoca/backends/casadi/model.py#L338
  Same same but different.
  ```Modelica
  Real x[3,1];
  ```
  would end up as as symbols named `x[1]`, `x[2]`, and `x[3]` in CasADi, instead of e.g. `x[1,1]` as a user would refer to it in Modelica.
- https://github.com/pymoca/pymoca/blob/de50f66ce80417a5dcc2c335533af038e0907cec/src/pymoca/backends/casadi/model.py#L348
  Value (e.g. a NumPy array) is converted to MX, and then we try both the symbol and its transpose and see if it fits.

issue_body:
If any contributor is feeling up for it, it would be good to add unit tests that check that appropriate error messages are getting raised in various situations not yet covered. It seems that a few error messages were previously incorrectly formatted, because we don't have unit tests for some of these exceptions.

We have *some* such unit tests already, which look like this:

```python
with pytest.raises(ValueError) as e_info:
      generator.flow((images, x_misc_err), np.arange(dsize), batch_size=3)
      assert 'All of the arrays in' in str(e_info.value)
```

[Upstream Bug and Solution]"TreeBuilderForHtml5lib 'module' object has no attribute '_base'"
issue_body:
if you use pip to install GeoPyTool and encounter the error:
> TreeBuilderForHtml5lib 'module' object has no attribute '_base'

You can try to run:
```Bash
pip install --upgrade html5lib==1.0b8
```
and then run the installation pip commands again.
That is caused by an issue with upstream package html5lib: https://bugs.launchpad.net/beautifulsoup/+bug/1603299To fix, force downgrade to an older version:
The information refered from https://github.com/coursera-dl/coursera-dl/issues/554


----------------------------------


如果你用pip来安装 geopytool的时候遇到了下面这样的报错:
> TreeBuilderForHtml5lib 'module' object has no attribute '_base'

可以试着运行:
```Bash
pip install --upgrade html5lib==1.0b8
```

然后再重新运行pip命令来安装.
这个问题是上游包html5lib的bug引起的.
这个解决方案参考了:https://github.com/coursera-dl/coursera-dl/issues/554

issue_title: --relocatable doesn't work with PyPy
issue_body:
To reproduce, create a new PyPy virtualenv

```
$ virtualenv2 -p pypy foo
Running virtualenv with interpreter /usr/bin/pypy
New pypy executable in foo/bin/pypy
Installing setuptools............done.
Installing pip...............done.
```

and try to make it relocatable:
```
$ virtualenv2 --relocatable foo
Script foo/bin/pip-2.7 cannot be made relative (it's not a normal script that starts with #!/tmp/foo/bin/python)
Script foo/bin/pip cannot be made relative (it's not a normal script that starts with #!/tmp/foo/bin/python)
[...]
```

The problem is that the shebang uses `pypy` not `python`:
```
$ head -n 1 foo/bin/pip
#!/tmp/foo/bin/pypy
```
